title: Pakistan — Application for Company Incorporation (Form-1), Single Member Company
description: The Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan's (SECP) Form-1, 'Application for Company Incorporation', filed under section 16 of the Companies Act, 2017, read with Regulations 8 and 30 of the Companies Regulations, 2024, via SECP's e-Services portal. Opens Pakistan's Business Formation vertical (Pakistan's first schema in this registry). Scoped to the simplest coherent pathway: a Single Member Company (SMC) limited by shares — exactly one natural person who is simultaneously the company's sole subscriber, sole director, and chief executive, together with the single mandatory nominee a Single Member Company must appoint. Out of scope: multi-subscriber/multi-director Private and Public Limited Companies (Form-1's own subscriber/director table, and its Ultimate Beneficial Owner table, are both open repeating tables sized for many rows; this version models exactly one subscriber-director-CEO, one nominee, and one direct natural-person UBO rather than a bounded or unbounded repeating group, since GovSchema's array/repeating-group proposal, GSP-0009, is not yet part of the accepted v0.3 specification), the 'Additional UBO information' table for indirect/layered beneficial ownership (Part IIA, Regulation 13(3)), companies limited by guarantee or unlimited companies (Section E's Table C/D/E pathways), the Minimum Subscription field (applicable only to Public Companies), and the witness-signature block (the form's own text states it applies 'Only for the documents submitted in physical form'; this schema assumes online e-Services filing, which the form itself says is signed electronically). It does not submit anything or imply Government of Pakistan endorsement.

authority.name: Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan

source.documentRef: Form-1, "Application for Company Incorporation", issued pursuant to section 16 of the Companies Act, 2017 read with Regulations 8 & 30 of the Companies Regulations, 2024

verification.notes: GOV-3062 'GovSchema Standard Research' cycle. Opens Pakistan as a new jurisdiction in this registry. A parallel scouting pass this cycle screened NADRA's CNIC application (dead end: only an 'attestation verification' request PDF is published, zero /AcroForm or /Widget markers, no extractable text layer over the actual application; the live CNIC application itself is filled in-person via NADRA's biometric system) and Punjab/Sindh Excise & Taxation vehicle-registration forms (dead end: all downloadable PDFs are scanned photocopies with near-zero extractable Tj text-show operators, field-level authoring not practical) before selecting this SECP form as the strongest verified candidate. Independently re-fetched this cycle from `secp.gov.pk`'s own Statutory Forms listing page (`https://www.secp.gov.pk/company-formation/formsapplications-schedule-of-filling-of-returns/statutory-forms/`, HTTP 200) via a WordPress Download Manager link, no login/CAPTCHA gate: HTTP 200, content-type `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`, 162,301 bytes, sha256 `5e34cf31a6590936726abe70fdfacb9c07e8c2ba32ecdec22430f4d27e5ac75c`. This is a native .docx (not PDF); unzipped directly and every `<w:t>` text run in `word/document.xml` was extracted and read in full (all three Parts, all lettered sections A–E, Part IIA, Part III, and the enclosures list), plus each of the form's data tables was parsed structurally (`<w:tbl>`/`<w:tr>`/`<w:tc>`) to confirm column headers, footnote markers, and the pre-numbered blank-row counts (the subscriber/director table's 17 lettered footnote markers (a)-(q) are attached one per column header, not per repeated row; the UBO table's own printed Sr.no. column runs 1 through 13 and the indirect-control UBO table's runs 1 through 10, confirming both are wide, many-row repeating tables in the live form). No `w:sdt`/`FORMTEXT` interactive form controls exist — this is a fill-in-the-blank template, not an interactive Word form, consistent with the byte-for-byte structure independently re-parsed this cycle. Given GSP-0009 (repeating groups/arrays) is not yet accepted into v0.3, and modeling the full open-ended subscriber/director and dual UBO tables would require either fabricating an arbitrary row cap not printed on the form or omitting the tables' own column semantics, this document is scoped to the Single Member Company (SMC) pathway instead: SECP's own minimum-directors table (2.9) states an SMC requires exactly one director, collapsing the subscriber/director/CEO table to a single natural person and the direct-ownership UBO table to that same person's own particulars — a real, commonly used Pakistani company type (not a toy example), and the only pathway in this form whose repeating tables reduce to exactly one row each without fabricating a bound. The mandatory Nominee block (2.11, required only for SMCs) is modeled in full, including its own literal relative-of-the-member enumerated list (spouse, father, mother, brother, sister, son, daughter) quoted verbatim from the form's own section heading. Field 2.10's "Designation (Director/Subscriber/CEO) Please specify" is modeled as a single-select enum using exactly the three literal labels printed on the form, even though in the SMC pathway one natural person functionally holds all three roles simultaneously — the schema captures the single value the source form's own "Please specify" instruction asks for, not an invented compound label. Gender is modeled as a free-text string, not an enum, because the form's own table column supplies no printed value list to draw from (no fabricated enum options). The registered-office/correspondence-address conditional relationship (2.1 vs. 2.2: correspondence address required only when no registered office address is given at incorporation) is disclosed here in prose rather than encoded as a `requiredWhen`, because doing so would require inventing a boolean gate field the source form does not itself print as a distinct checkbox; both address blocks are instead modeled with `required: false`, matching 2.1's own "if any" qualifier. `documents[]` (7 entries) is derived directly from the form's own numbered Enclosures list and Part III declaration text (quoted verbatim into the `attestation` entry's `statement`), with the custom-articles-of-association and specialized-business-NOC entries gated via `requiredWhen` on `articlesOfAssociationTable`/`isSpecializedBusinessRequiringLicense` matching the form's own conditional instructions ('if the company has not adopted the Articles contained in First Schedule...it shall attach the articles of association'; 'Applicable in case of Banking Company...or any other business subject to license'). An ephemeral, from-scratch conformance checker (deriving required/requiredWhen/enum rules directly from this schema's own fields[]/documents[], discarded after use, not committed) ran 1 valid mock scenario (a Single Member Company incorporation with a spouse nominee) at 0 errors, plus 5 mutation-control fixtures (missing required field, missing required document, enum violation, requiredWhen violation on the custom-articles document, invalid date format) each correctly raising exactly 1 error.

fields.name: companyName
fields.label: Name of the Company
fields.description: The proposed name of the company being incorporated.
fields.sourceRef: Part I, 1.1 — "Name of the Company"

fields.name: challanNumber
fields.label: Challan No
fields.description: Reference number of the bank challan (or e-Services equivalent) evidencing payment of the incorporation fee.
fields.sourceRef: Part I, 1.2.1 — "Challan No"

fields.name: challanAmount
fields.label: Challan Amount (Rs.)
fields.description: Amount paid, in Pakistani Rupees, per the fee challan.
fields.sourceRef: Part I, 1.2.2 — "Challan Amount (Rs.)"

fields.name: registeredOfficeAddressLine
fields.label: Registered office Address
fields.description: Street/building address of the company's registered office, if given at the time of incorporation. If not given, a Correspondence Address (below) is required instead.
fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.1 — "Registered office Address, if any"

fields.label: Registered office — Email Address
fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.1 — "Email Address"

fields.name: registeredOfficeMobileNumber
fields.label: Mobile Number of the Chief Executive/Secretary/any director
fields.description: Mobile number of the Chief Executive, Secretary, or any director, preferably WhatsApp-enabled. For a Single Member Company this is the sole director/CEO's own mobile number.
fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.1 — "Mobile Number of the Chief Executive/Secretary/any director (Preferably Whatsapp enabled number)"

fields.name: financialYearEnd
fields.label: Financial Year End
fields.description: The company's chosen financial year-end date (day and month, recurring annually).
fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.1 — "Financial Year End"

fields.name: correspondenceAddressLine
fields.label: Correspondence Address
fields.description: Only to be provided if the company has not given a registered office address at the time of incorporation (2.1 above).
fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.2 — "Correspondence Address"

fields.label: Whether the company is a wholly owned subsidiary of the Federal Government

fields.sourceRef: Part II, Section A, 2.4 — "Principal line of business (Brief objects as per clause 3 (i) of the Memorandum may be mentioned) including additional clauses (up to 3), if any."

fields.name: isSpecializedBusinessRequiringLicense
fields.label: Special business information applicable
fields.description: Whether the company's business is one requiring a license/permission/approval under Section C (e.g. banking, non-banking finance, insurance, modaraba management, stock brokerage, forex, clearing house, or a business otherwise restricted/licensed by law).

documents.label: Original challan or other evidence of payment of fee
documents.sourceRef: Enclosures — "Original challan or other evidence of payment of fee specified in Seventh Schedule of the Act (not applicable in case of online filing)"

documents.label: Declaration under section 16
documents.statement: I do hereby solemnly and sincerely declare that: a) I have been authorized as declarant by the subscribers; b) all the requirements of the Companies Act, 2017, and the regulations made there under in respect of matters precedent to the registration of the said Company and incidental thereto have been complied with. c) necessary information about the ultimate beneficial owners of the proposed company, if any, as specified in regulation 13, has been obtained and is available on record. d) I make this solemn declaration conscientiously believing the same to be true.
documents.sourceRef: Part III, 3.3 — "DeclarationI do hereby solemnly and sincerely declare that..."
